metadata.title: OpenCore Legacy Patcher Compatibility Checker
metadata.description: Use an unofficial OCLP compatibility planning checker with links to Dortania supported models and troubleshooting sources.

checker.title: Check an unsupported Mac before planning an OCLP upgrade
checker.description: Search a common Mac model identifier, review the planning note, then verify the exact model in the official Dortania supported models documentation before changing a machine.

workflow.title: Compatibility planning workflow
workflow.description: Treat this as a checklist before reading the official install and troubleshooting docs.

items.title: Find the exact identifier
items.description: Use System Information to confirm a model identifier such as MacBookPro11,1, not just the marketing name.

items.title: Compare official support
items.description: Open the Dortania supported models page and confirm the model appears in the current documentation.

items.title: Read hardware caveats
items.description: Graphics, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and post-install root patches can change the risk profile.

items.title: Back up first
items.description: Do not treat a compatibility note as a guarantee. Back up and read current release notes before upgrading.

faq.title: OpenCore Legacy Patcher compatibility FAQ
faq.description: Limits and source boundaries for this independent checker.

items.question: Is this an official OpenCore Legacy Patcher checker?
items.answer: No. Motubrain.org is independent and links to official Dortania resources for verification.

items.question: Does a likely-supported result guarantee the upgrade will work?
items.answer: No. Hardware condition, firmware, GPU, wireless cards, and current OCLP release notes can change the outcome.

items.question: Can I download OCLP from this site?
items.answer: No. This site does not mirror downloads. Use the official OCLP documentation and GitHub repository.
